aliases: Steamed Buns with Red Bean Filling

english: Steamed Buns with Red Bean Filling

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general steamed bun class (FOODON_03302397); no specific red bean filled steamed bun entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJX covers steamed bread and buns as a category; no specific red bean filled variant exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept exists for steamed red bean buns. No suitable parent concept identified for this food product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of steamed red bean buns consists of 3 pieces, each approximately 60 g, totalling approximately 180 g as typically served at Chinese dim sum restaurants.

nutrient_source.title: Steamed bun with sweet filling (豆沙包) — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / FAO INFOODS adapted composite
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central; cross-referenced with FAO/INFOODS East Asian food composition data
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA FoodData Central entry exactly matches Din Tai Fung-style steamed red bean buns. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central entry for 'Bread, steamed, Chinese style' (NDB 18009) and 'Beans, adzuki, cooked' (NDB 16001), weighted to reflect approximately 55% dough and 45% sweetened red bean paste filling by mass, consistent with published Chinese dim sum composition references. Energy and macros cross-checked against FAO/INFOODS SEA food composition tables for steamed filled buns.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of steamed wheat-flour buns with sweet filling — analogous estimate based on steamed white bread and sweetened bean paste GI data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for steamed red bean buns (豆沙包) in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on: (1) steamed white bread GI of approximately 68 (University of Sydney GI Database, reference food); (2) sweetened adzuki bean paste GI of approximately 40–50 (published peer-reviewed data: Sugiyama et al., 2003, Am J Clin Nutr); weighted composite reflecting approximately 55% wheat dough and 45% sweetened red bean paste filling yields an estimated GI of 65. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (65 × 90.9 g available carbohydrate per 180 g serving) / 100 = 59.1.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content and refined carbohydrate load
hypertension.guidance: Each 180 g serving provides approximately 378 mg sodium; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size and avoid pairing with other high-sodium dim sum dishes to stay within the recommended 2000 mg daily sodium limit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load and refined carbohydrate content with added sugar in red bean paste
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a glycemic load of approximately 59 per 3-piece serving and 14 g sugar per 100 g, steamed red bean buns can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit to 1 piece per occasion and pair with protein or non-starchy vegetables to blunt glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat and zero cholesterol, but moderate refined carbohydrate and sugar
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Steamed red bean buns are low in saturated fat (0.9 g per 100 g) and contain no cholesterol, making them relatively heart-friendly in small portions; however, the moderate sugar content and refined flour base warrant portion control as part of a heart-healthy dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Steamed red bean buns (豆沙包, dòu shā bāo) are a classic Chinese dim sum and bakery item consisting of a soft, fluffy steamed wheat-flour dough encasing a smooth, sweetened red bean (adzuki bean) paste filling. The dough is leavened with yeast and steamed rather than baked, producing a characteristically white, pillowy exterior with a slightly chewy yet tender crumb. The filling is made from cooked adzuki beans mashed and sweetened with sugar, sometimes enriched with a small amount of fat to achieve a smooth, glossy texture. Each bun weighs approximately 55–65 g and is typically served in sets of three at Chinese restaurants and dim sum establishments. The dish is popular across China, Taiwan, Hong Kong, and Chinese diaspora communities in Singapore and Southeast Asia. Nutritionally, steamed red bean buns are a moderate-energy food providing approximately 255 kcal per 100 g, with carbohydrates as the dominant macronutrient (50.5 g per 100 g), moderate protein (6.2 g), and low fat (2.8 g). The red bean filling contributes dietary fibre (2.8 g per 100 g) and plant-based iron. The glycemic index is estimated at 65 (medium), and a 3-piece serving carries a high glycemic load of approximately 59, making portion control important for individuals managing blood glucose. Sodium content is moderate at approximately 210 mg per 100 g. The dish is vegetarian, dairy-free, and halal-suitable, but contains gluten. It is not suitable for individuals with coeliac disease or wheat allergy.

confidence_reason: No exact match for Din Tai Fung steamed red bean buns exists in any approved primary source database. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central steamed bread and cooked adzuki bean entries, weighted by estimated dough-to-filling ratio. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database reference foods. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component foods are well-characterised in approved sources and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Serving size of 180 g (3 × 60 g pieces) is based on the field survey listing for Din Tai Fung (3pcs) and is consistent with typical dim sum portion sizes reported in published food composition studies. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero: steamed buns made with vegetable shortening or lard contain negligible industrially produced trans fats; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central steamed bread reference. 3. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero: no animal-derived ingredients with significant cholesterol are present in standard red bean bun formulations (no egg, no dairy); confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central adzuki bean and steamed bread references. 4. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero: wheat flour and red bean paste contain no meaningful vitamin C; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 5. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero: this is a plant-based food with no animal-derived ingredients; vitamin B12 is naturally zero. 6. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 — not reported in the adapted USDA FoodData Central composite for this food type. 7. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 — not reported in the adapted source composite. 8. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 — not reported in the adapted source composite. 9. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 — not reported in the adapted source composite. 10. vitamin_e_mg is -1 — not reported in the adapted source composite. 11. selenium_mcg is -1 — not reported in the adapted source composite. 12. water_g is 38.0 — derived from USDA FoodData Central adapted composite. 13. GI glycemic load per serving calculated as (65 × 90.9) / 100 = 59.1, where available carbohydrate per 180 g serving = 50.5 × 1.8 = 90.9 g. 14. Vegan flag is false because commercial red bean paste preparations may contain lard; vegetarian flag is true as plant-based versions are common. 15. The cuisine tag 'Noodles' in the original field survey input is not applicable to this food and has been disregarded.
